name: No barrel exports in packages
instruction: Package source files must use explicit named exports, not barrel exports (export * from). Barrel exports break tree-shaking and can silently break RSC/client boundaries in production builds.
relevant_when: TypeScript or TSX file under packages/ that contains an export statement re-exporting from another module (not a test file, not a type-only re-export).
context: Barrel exports (export * from) cause production bundling issues where client code gets evaluated in server context, breaking React Server Components. They also defeat tree-shaking, increasing bundle size.
scope_reason: Each file's export statements can be inspected independently.

checklist.name: no-barrel-reexport
checklist.rule: The file does not use 'export * from' syntax to re-export from another module. Explicit named exports like 'export { Foo } from' or 'export type * from' (type-only) are acceptable.
